questions.question: After obtaining a reverse shell, a penetration tester identifies a locally cloned Git repository that contains thousands of files and directories on a Windows machine. The tester suspects there could Questions & Answers PDF P-253 https://www.pass4success.com be sensitive information related to "ProjectX." Which of the following commands should the tester use in a script to identify potential files to produce the best results?
questions.explanation:
Comprehensive and Detailed
On Windows PowerShell, gci is an alias for Get-ChildItem. To search recursively through all files and
return matches for the string "ProjectX", the combination gci -Path . -Recurse | Select-String -Pattern
"ProjectX" is efficient and returns file paths and matching lines. This handles large repositories and
searches file contents rather than just file names.
Why D over C/B/A:
C (Get-ChildItem * | Select-String "ProjectX"): Works but lacks -Recurse so it may not descend into
subdirectories unless Get-ChildItem is invoked with -Recurse.
B (dir /R | findstr): dir /R lists alternate data streams; it does not reliably search file contents and is
less robust for large repos.
A (gc * | select "ProjectX"): gc (Get-Content) on * could attempt to load huge files into memory and
select "ProjectX" is not a correct PowerShell pattern for searching content.
PT0-003 mapping: Domain 4 — using scripting/PowerShell to efficiently locate sensitive strings in
large code/data sets.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt Informationen über die PCI-Geräte des Systems an?
questions.explanation: lspci listet alle PCI-Bus-Geräte und ihre Konfiguration. lsusb zeigt USB-Geräte, lscpu zeigt CPU-Informationen.

questions.question: Welche Datei enthält Informationen über den verbauten Arbeitsspeicher (RAM)?
questions.explanation: /proc/meminfo zeigt detaillierte Informationen über den Systemspeicher, /proc/cpuinfo über die CPU.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt USB-Geräte mit detaillierten Informationen an?
questions.explanation: lsusb -v zeigt verbose Informationen über alle angeschlossenen USB-Geräte.

questions.question: Wo wird die Boot-Reihenfolge im BIOS/UEFI typischerweise konfiguriert?
questions.explanation: Die Boot-Reihenfolge wird im BIOS/UEFI-Setup konfiguriert, nicht im Betriebssystem.

questions.question: Welcher Runlevel wird für den Single-User-Mode (Wartungsmodus) in SysVinit verwendet?
questions.explanation: Runlevel 1 ist Single-User-Mode. 0=Shutdown, 3=Multiuser mit Netz, 5=Multiuser mit GUI.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl schaltet das System sofort aus?
questions.explanation: shutdown -h now hält das System sofort an (-h = halt). poweroff fährt ebenfalls herunter.

questions.question: Welche Datei wird vom Kernel beim Booten als initiales RAM-Dateisystem geladen?
questions.explanation: initramfs (oder initrd) ist das initiale RAM-Dateisystem, das beim Booten geladen wird. vmlinuz ist der Kernel.

questions.question: Welches systemd-Target entspricht dem klassischen Runlevel 3 (Multiuser mit Netzwerk)?
questions.explanation: multi-user.target entspricht Runlevel 3. graphical.target = Runlevel 5, rescue.target = Runlevel 1.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt die Belegung der Festplattenpartitionen an?
questions.explanation: df -h zeigt die Festplattenbelegung (disk free) human-readable. du zeigt Verzeichnisgrößen.

questions.question: Mit welchem Befehl wird ein neues ext4-Dateisystem erstellt?
questions.explanation: mkfs.ext4 erstellt ein ext4-Dateisystem. fsck prüft Dateisysteme, mount hängt sie ein.

questions.question: Wo werden die Dateisysteme definiert, die beim Booten automatisch eingehängt werden?
questions.explanation: /etc/fstab (file systems table) definiert die permanenten Mountpoints.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl installiert ein Paket unter Debian/Ubuntu?
questions.explanation: apt ist der Paketmanager für Debian/Ubuntu. yum/dnf sind für RHEL/CentOS/Fedora.

questions.question: Wie wird ein RPM-Paket unter RHEL/CentOS installiert?
questions.explanation: rpm -ivh installiert (i) mit verbose (v) und Hash-Fortschritt (h). -e entfernt, -q fragt ab.

questions.question: Welches Tool wird verwendet, um Logical Volumes (LVM) zu verwalten?
questions.explanation: LVM verwendet pvcreate (Physical Volume), vgcreate (Volume Group), lvcreate (Logical Volume).

questions.question: Welcher Befehl hängt alle in /etc/fstab definierten Dateisysteme ein?
questions.explanation: mount -a mountet alle in /etc/fstab definierten Einträge.

questions.question: Wie zeigt man den freien Speicherplatz eines Verzeichnisses an?
questions.explanation: df -h zeigt den freien Speicherplatz des Dateisystems. du zeigt die belegte Größe eines Verzeichnisses.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt die letzten 10 Zeilen einer Datei an?
questions.explanation: tail zeigt das Ende einer Datei. head zeigt den Anfang. -n 10 begrenzt auf 10 Zeilen.

questions.question: Wie sucht man rekursiv nach einer Datei namens 'config.txt'?
questions.explanation: find / -name sucht ab / rekursiv. locate verwacht eine Datenbank, grep sucht in Dateiinhalten.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl ersetzt 'alt' durch 'neu' in allen Zeilen einer Datei?
questions.explanation: sed 's/alt/neu/g' ersetzt (s=substitute) global (g). tr ersetzt einzelne Zeichen, nicht Strings.

questions.question: Wie werden die Standardausgabe (stdout) und Standardfehler (stderr) in eine Datei umgeleitet?
questions.explanation: > datei 2>&1 leitet stdout (> datei) und stderr (2>&1 = auf die gleiche Stelle wie Dateideskriptor 1) um.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zählt die Zeilen, Wörter und Zeichen einer Datei?
questions.explanation: wc (word count) zählt Zeilen (-l), Wörter (-w) und Bytes (-c). nl nummeriert Zeilen.

questions.question: Wie zeigt man die laufenden Prozesse des aktuellen Benutzers an?
questions.explanation: ps zeigt Prozesse, top/htop zeigen dynamische Prozesslisten. Alle sind korrekt.

questions.question: Welches Signal beendet einen Prozess (default bei kill)?
questions.explanation: SIGTERM (15) ist das Standard-Signal, das einen Prozess sauber beendet. SIGKILL erzwingt die Beendigung.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl ändert die Berechtigungen einer Datei auf rwxr-xr-x?
questions.explanation: 755 = rwxr-xr-x (Eigentümer: alles, Gruppe+Andere: lesen+ausführen). 644 = rw-r--r--.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt die erste Spalte einer CSV-Datei (mit Komma als Trennzeichen)?
questions.explanation: Beide cut und awk können Spalten extrahieren. cut -d',' -f1 und awk -F',' '{print $1}' sind äquivalent.

questions.question: Wie sortiert man eine Datei numerisch aufsteigend?
questions.explanation: sort -n sortiert numerisch. -r ist reverse, -k spezifiziert Schlüsselfelder, -u entfernt Duplikate.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt Zeilennummern an, wenn er Dateien anzeigt?
questions.explanation: cat -n, nl und grep -n zeigen alle Zeilennummern an, jeweils mit unterschiedlichem Kontext.

questions.question: Was macht der Befehl 'tar -czvf archiv.tar.gz /pfad'?
options: C. Listet den Inhalt eines tar-Archivs
questions.explanation: tar -czvf: c=create, z=gzip, v=verbose, f=file. Erstellt ein komprimiertes Archiv.

questions.question: Wie werden alle Dateien im aktuellen Verzeichnis mit der Endung .log gelöscht?
questions.explanation: rm *.log löscht alle .log-Dateien. delete/remove sind keine Standard-Linux-Befehle.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt den aktuellen Arbeitsverzeichnispfad an?
questions.explanation: pwd (print working directory) zeigt das aktuelle Verzeichnis. cd wechselt Verzeichnisse.

questions.question: Wie wird ein Hardlink erstellt?
questions.explanation: ln ohne Option erstellt einen Hardlink. ln -s erstellt einen symbolischen Link (Symlink).

questions.question: Welcher reguläre Ausdruck matcht eine beliebige Ziffer?
questions.explanation: [0-9] matcht eine beliebige Ziffer. \d funktioniert in grep mit -P (Perl-regexp).

questions.question: Welches Verzeichnis enthält die Konfigurationsdateien des Systems?
questions.explanation: /etc enthält systemweite Konfigurationsdateien. /var variable Daten, /usr Benutzerprogramme, /opt optionale Software.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl prüft ein ext4-Dateisystem auf Fehler?
questions.explanation: fsck (file system check) prüft und repariert Dateisysteme. -t ext4 spezifiziert den Typ.

questions.question: Welches Verzeichnis enthält variable Daten wie Logs und Spool-Dateien?
questions.explanation: /var enthält variable Daten wie Logs, Spool-Dateien und temporäre Daten.

questions.question: Wo befinden sich die Home-Verzeichnisse der Benutzer?
questions.explanation: /home ist der Standardort für Benutzer-Homeverzeichnisse im FHS (Filesystem Hierarchy Standard).

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt den belegten Speicherplatz eines Verzeichnisbaums an?
questions.explanation: du -sh (disk usage, summarize, human-readable) zeigt die Größe eines Verzeichnisbaums.

questions.question: Welches Verzeichnis enthält die Kernel-Module?
questions.explanation: /lib/modules/$(uname -r) enthält die Kernel-Module für den aktuellen Kernel.

questions.question: Welche Datei enthält die Mount-Informationen des aktuellen Systems?
questions.explanation: Alle drei enthalten Mount-Informationen: /proc/mounts (Kernel), /etc/fstab (Konfiguration), /etc/mtab (aktuell).

questions.question: Welche Dateisysteme unterstützen Journaling standardmäßig?
questions.explanation: ext3, ext4 und XFS unterstützen Journaling. ext2 nicht, FAT32/NTFS sind nicht-native Linux-FS.

questions.question: Welche Datei wird bei der Anmeldung einer interaktiven Bash-Login-Shell ausgeführt?
questions.explanation: Login-Shells lesen /etc/profile und ~/.bash_profile (oder ~/.profile). ~/.bashrc wird von interaktiven Non-Login-Shells gelesen.

questions.question: Wie wird eine Variable in der Bash als exportiert (für Kind-Prozesse verfügbar) markiert?
questions.explanation: export macht eine Variable für Kind-Prozesse verfügbar. set zeigt alle Variablen an, env zeigt Umgebungsvariablen.

questions.question: Welche Schleife iteriert über eine Liste von Werten in Bash?
options: A. for i in wert1 wert2 wert3; do ... done
C. foreach i (wert1 wert2); do ... done
D. loop i in wert1 wert2; do ... done
questions.explanation: for i in ... ist die Standard-Bash-Schleife für Listen. foreach existiert nicht in Bash.

questions.question: Wie wird der Rückgabewert (Exit-Code) des letzten Befehls in der Bash abgefragt?
questions.explanation: $? enthält den Exit-Code des letzten Befehls. $! ist die PID des letzten Hintergrundprozesses, $$ die aktuelle PID.

questions.question: Welche Shebang-Zeile gehört in ein Bash-Skript?
options: C. Beide A und B sind korrekt
questions.explanation: #!/bin/bash und #!/usr/bin/env bash sind beide gültige Shebangs. env ist portabler.

questions.question: Wie schreibt man eine bedingte Anweisung in Bash?
questions.explanation: if [ ]; then ... fi ist die korrekte Bash-Syntax. case ... esac ist für Muster.

questions.question: Wie wird ein Kommentar in einem Bash-Skript geschrieben?
questions.explanation: # leitet in Bash einen Kommentar ein. // und /* */ sind für C/Java.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl gibt die Umgebungsvariablen aus?
questions.explanation: env zeigt exportierte Variablen, export zeigt exportierte Variablen, set zeigt alle Variablen.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl erstellt einen neuen Benutzer mit Home-Verzeichnis?
questions.explanation: useradd -m erstellt einen Benutzer mit Home (-m). adduser (Debian) ist ein interaktives Frontend.

questions.question: Welche Datei enthält die verschlüsselten Benutzerpasswörter?
questions.explanation: /etc/shadow enthält die verschlüsselten Passwörter. /etc/passwd enthält nur Benutzerinformationen (lesbar für alle).

questions.question: Welcher Befehl ändert das Passwort eines Benutzers?
questions.explanation: passwd ändert Passwörter. Als root kann man jedes Passwort ändern.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl fügt einen Benutzer zu einer Gruppe hinzu?
questions.explanation: usermod -aG (append, Group) fügt einen Benutzer zu einer Gruppe hinzu, ohne bestehende zu entfernen.

questions.question: Welche Datei definiert die Standard-Umask für neue Benutzer?
questions.explanation: /etc/login.defs enthält Standardeinstellungen wie UMASK. /etc/profile enthält Shell-Standards.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt die Gruppenmitgliedschaften eines Benutzers an?
questions.explanation: groups zeigt Gruppennamen, id -G zeigt GIDs. Beide zeigen Gruppenmitgliedschaften.

questions.question: Was steht in der /etc/passwd-Datei für jeden Benutzer?
questions.explanation: /etc/passwd hat das Format: name:password:UID:GID:GECOS:home_directory:shell. Das Passwort-Feld enthält x (in /etc/shadow).

questions.question: Welcher Befehl löscht einen Benutzer inklusive Home-Verzeichnis?
questions.explanation: userdel -r (remove) löscht Benutzer und Home. deluser --remove-home (Debian) macht dasselbe.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt die aktuelle Systemzeit an?
questions.explanation: date zeigt die aktuelle Systemzeit. timedatectl (systemd) zeigt mehr Details inkl. Zeitzone.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt Informationen über die System-Logs an (systemd)?
questions.explanation: journalctl ist das systemd-Log-Tool. dmesg zeigt Kernel-Logs, syslog ist der traditionelle Daemon.

questions.question: Wie startet man einen systemd-Service?
questions.explanation: Alle drei Methoden funktionieren, aber systemctl ist der moderne systemd-Weg.

questions.question: Wie aktiviert man einen Service, damit er beim Booten startet?
questions.explanation: systemctl enable erstellt Symlinks für den Boot-Start. start startet sofort, enable sorgt für Autostart.

questions.question: Wie wird ein Cron-Job erstellt, der jeden Tag um 2:30 Uhr läuft?
questions.explanation: Cron-Format: Minute Stunde Tag Monat Wochentag. 30 2 * * * = täglich um 2:30.

questions.question: Welche Datei enthält die systemweiten Cron-Jobs?
questions.explanation: /etc/crontab ist systemweit, /var/spool/cron/ für Benutzer, /etc/cron.d/ für Pakete.

questions.question: Welcher Befehl zeigt die Netzwerkkonfiguration an?
questions.explanation: ip addr (moderner Weg), ifconfig (traditionell), nmcli (NetworkManager) zeigen alle Netzwerk-Interfaces.

questions.question: Wie wird der Hostname temporär (bis zum Neustart) geändert?
questions.explanation: hostname ändert temporär. hostnamectl set-hostname ändert permanent (persistiert).

questions.question: Which command displays the current systemd target?
questions.explanation: systemctl get-default shows the current systemd target (e.g., graphical.target, multi-user.target).

questions.question: Which file stores the system's static hostname in systemd-based distributions?
questions.explanation: /etc/hostname stores the static hostname in systemd distributions. hostnamectl reads and writes this file.

questions.question: Which command creates a new LVM logical volume named 'data' of 10GB in volume group 'vg0'?
questions.explanation: lvcreate -L 10G sets size, -n data names it, vg0 is the volume group.

questions.question: Which command resizes an ext4 filesystem after extending its logical volume?
questions.explanation: resize2fs resizes ext2/3/4 filesystems. For online resizing, use resize2fs on a mounted filesystem.

questions.question: Where is the EFI system partition typically mounted?
questions.explanation: The EFI System Partition (ESP) is typically mounted at /boot/efi in Linux systems.

questions.question: Which command shows the UUID of all block devices?
questions.explanation: blkid shows UUIDs, filesystem types, and labels. lsblk -o UUID also shows UUIDs for block devices.

questions.question: Which GRUB2 configuration file should be edited to make persistent boot changes?
questions.explanation: /etc/default/grub is the configuration file. After editing, run grub2-mkconfig to regenerate grub.cfg.

questions.question: Which command regenerates the GRUB2 configuration after editing /etc/default/grub?
questions.explanation: grub2-mkconfig (RHEL) and update-grub (Debian) both regenerate the GRUB configuration.

questions.question: Which command sets SELinux to permissive mode?
questions.explanation: setenforce 0 sets SELinux to permissive (1=enforcing). sestatus only shows the current status.

questions.question: Which file contains the SELinux configuration for the permanent mode?
questions.explanation: /etc/selinux/config is the main config. /etc/sysconfig/selinux is often a symlink to it on RHEL.

questions.question: Which command generates SSH key pairs?
questions.explanation: ssh-keygen generates SSH public/private key pairs. Default location: ~/.ssh/id_rsa or id_ed25519.

questions.question: Which command displays the status of AppArmor for all profiles?
questions.explanation: aa-status shows the status of all AppArmor profiles (enforce, complain, or disabled).

questions.question: Which nftables table family is used for IPv4 traffic?
questions.explanation: The 'ip' family in nftables handles IPv4. 'inet' handles both IPv4 and IPv6.

questions.question: Which PAM module is used for password complexity requirements?
questions.explanation: pam_pwquality (successor to pam_cracklib) enforces password quality requirements in PAM.

questions.question: Which command shows the SELinux context of files?
questions.explanation: ls -Z shows SELinux security contexts. -Z is the context flag for ls, ps, and other tools.

questions.question: Where are the sudo privilege rules defined?
questions.explanation: /etc/sudoers contains sudo rules. Edit with visudo to ensure syntax checking.

questions.question: Which command reloads a systemd service configuration without restarting?
questions.explanation: systemctl reload reloads configuration. restart stops and starts the service.

questions.question: Which file configures the DNS resolver in modern systemd-based systems?
questions.explanation: systemd-resolved manages DNS via /etc/systemd/resolved.conf. /etc/resolv.conf may be a symlink.

questions.question: Which command shows listening TCP ports with process information?
questions.explanation: ss (modern), netstat (traditional), and lsof can all show listening ports with process info.

questions.question: Which Apache configuration directive defines the document root?
questions.explanation: DocumentRoot in Apache defines the directory from which web content is served.

questions.question: Which command creates a systemd timer unit that runs a service weekly?
options: A. OnCalendar=weekly in the timer unit
C. Weekly=yes in the service unit
questions.explanation: OnCalendar=weekly in a .timer unit schedules weekly execution. systemd timers replace cron.

questions.question: Which directive in nginx.conf defines the server name?
questions.explanation: server_name in nginx defines the hostname for a virtual server.

questions.question: Which command shows the current mail queue in Postfix?
questions.explanation: mailq and postqueue -p both display the Postfix mail queue.

questions.question: Which DHCP server configuration file is used on Debian/Ubuntu?
questions.explanation: /etc/dhcp/dhcpd.conf is the ISC DHCP server configuration file on Debian/Ubuntu systems.

questions.question: Which Ansible module is used to install packages?
questions.explanation: The 'package' module is the generic module that works across distributions (apt, yum, dnf).

questions.question: Which Ansible command runs a playbook?
questions.explanation: ansible-playbook executes Ansible playbooks. ansible runs ad-hoc commands.

questions.question: Which command traces system calls made by a process?
questions.explanation: strace traces system calls. ltrace traces library calls. Both are essential debugging tools.

questions.question: Which command shows the last 20 system boot messages?
questions.explanation: dmesg shows kernel ring buffer messages. journalctl --boot shows boot logs from the current boot.

questions.question: Which command shows network interface statistics and errors?
questions.explanation: ip -s link, ifconfig, and ethtool can all display interface statistics and error counters.

questions.question: Which command shows the routing table?
questions.explanation: ip route (modern), route -n (traditional), and netstat -r all display the routing table.

questions.question: Which file contains the kernel messages log?
questions.explanation: Kernel messages go to /var/log/kern.log, /var/log/messages (RHEL), or /var/log/syslog (Debian).

questions.question: Which command shows the I/O statistics per block device?
questions.explanation: iostat shows I/O statistics, iotop shows per-process I/O, blktrace traces block I/O operations.

questions.question: A penetration tester is unable to identify the Wi-Fi SSID on a client's cell phone. Which of the following techniques would be most effective to troubleshoot this issue?
questions.explanation: Since SSID broadcast might be hidden, channel scanning allows the tester to identify active Wi-Fi networks. Option A (Sidecar scanning): Not a recognized Wi-Fi testing method. Option B (Channel scanning): Correct. Identifies hidden SSIDs by monitoring probe requests and responses. Option C (Stealth scanning): Typically refers to evading detection, not Wi-Fi analysis. Option D (Static analysis scanning): Static analysis applies to code security, not Wi-Fi networks.

questions.question: During an assessment, a penetration tester gains access to one of the internal hosts. Given the following command: schtasks /create /sc onlogon /tn "Windows Update" /tr "cmd.exe /c reverse_shell.exe" Which of the following is the penetration tester trying to do with this code?
options: C. Deactivate the Windows Update functionality
D. Create a binary application for Windows System Updates
questions.explanation: The command creates a scheduled task that executes a reverse shell payload at logon, ensuring persistence. Option A (Enumerate tasks): This command creates a task, not lists tasks (schtasks /query is used for enumeration). Option B (Establish persistence): Correct. The attacker ensures a reverse shell opens every time a user logs in. Option C (Deactivate Windows Update): The task is named 'Windows Update' but does not disable updates. Option D (Create a Windows Update binary): This executes a reverse shell, not a system update.

questions.question: During a routine penetration test, the client's security team observes logging alerts that indicate several ID badges were reprinted after working hours without authorization. Which of the following is the penetration tester most likely trying to do?
options: A. Obtain long-term, valid access to the facility
B. Disrupt the availability of facility access systems
C. Change access to the facility for valid users
D. Revoke access to the facility for valid users
questions.explanation: The unauthorized reprinting of ID badges suggests the penetration tester is attempting physical security penetration testing to gain long-term access. Option A (Obtain long-term, valid access): Correct. Cloning or reprinting badges allows persistent access past security checks. Option B (Disrupt availability): There is no indication of a denial-of-service attack. Option C (Change access for valid users): The goal is not modifying user access, but rather gaining unauthorized access. Option D (Revoke access for valid users): The logs show new badges being printed, not revocation.

questions.question: A penetration tester performs an assessment on the target company's Kubernetes cluster using kube-hunter. Which of the following types of vulnerabilities could be detected with the tool?
options: A. Network configuration errors in Kubernetes services
B. Weaknesses and misconfigurations in the Kubernetes cluster
C. Application deployment issues in Kubernetes
D. Security vulnerabilities specific to Docker containers
questions.explanation: kube-hunter is a tool designed to perform security assessments on Kubernetes clusters. It identifies various vulnerabilities, focusing on weaknesses and misconfigurations. Kube-hunter scans Kubernetes clusters to identify security issues, such as misconfigurations, insecure settings, and potential attack vectors. Network Configuration Errors: While kube-hunter might identify some network-related issues, its primary focus is on Kubernetes-specific vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.

questions.question: A penetration tester initiated the transfer of a large data set to verify a proof-of-concept attack as permitted by the ROE. The tester noticed the client's data included PII, which is out of scope, and immediately stopped the transfer. Which of the following MOST likely explains the penetration tester's decision?
options: A. The tester had the situational awareness to stop the transfer.
B. The tester found evidence of prior compromise within the data set.
C. The tester completed the assigned part of the assessment workflow.
D. The tester reached the end of the assessment time frame.
questions.explanation: Situational awareness is the ability to perceive and understand the environment and events around oneself, and to act accordingly. The penetration tester demonstrated situational awareness by stopping the transfer of PII, which was out of scope and could have violated the ROE or legal and ethical principles.

questions.question: A security engineer is trying to bypass a network IPS that isolates the source when the scan exceeds 100 packets per minute. The scope of the scan is to identify web servers in the 10.0.0.0/16 subnet. Which of the following commands should the engineer use to achieve the objective in the least amount of time?
questions.explanation: The nmap -T4 -p 80 10.0.0.0/16 --max-rate 60 command is used to scan the 10.0.0.0/16 subnet for web servers (port 80) at a maximum rate of 60 packets per minute. The -T4 option sets the timing template to 'aggressive', which speeds up the scan. The --max-rate option limits the number of packets sent per second, helping to bypass the network IPS that isolates the source when the scan exceeds 100 packets per minute.

questions.question: During passive reconnaissance of a target organization's infrastructure, a penetration tester wants to identify key contacts and job responsibilities within the company. Which of the following techniques would be the most effective for this situation?
questions.explanation: Social media scraping involves collecting information from social media platforms where employees might share their roles, responsibilities, and professional affiliations. This method can reveal detailed insights into the organizational structure, key personnel, and specific job functions within the target organization, making it an invaluable tool for understanding the company's internal landscape without alerting the target to the reconnaissance activities.

questions.question: A penetration tester is performing an assessment against a customer's web application that is hosted in a major cloud provider's environment. The penetration tester observes that the majority of the attacks attempted are being blocked by the organization's WAF. Which of the following attacks would be most likely to succeed?
questions.explanation: Direct-to-origin attacks bypass the WAF by targeting the origin server directly rather than going through the WAF-protected entry point. If the WAF is blocking most attacks, targeting the origin server directly (if its IP is known) would be the most likely to succeed since it circumvents the WAF protection entirely.

questions.question: Which of the following tools would be best to use to conceal data in various kinds of image files?
questions.explanation: Snow is a tool designed for steganography, which is the practice of concealing messages or information within other non-secret text or data. Snow is specifically used to hide data within whitespace of text files, which can include the whitespace areas of images saved in formats that support text descriptions or metadata.

questions.question: A penetration tester ran a simple Python-based scanner. Which of the following BEST describes why this script triggered a 'probable port scan' alert in the organization's IDS?
options: A. sock.settimeout(20) on line 7 caused each next socket to be created every 20 milliseconds.
B. *range(1, 1025) on line 1 populated the portList list in numerical order.
C. Line 6 uses socket.SOCK_STREAM instead of socket.SOCK_DGRAM
D. The remoteSvr variable has neither been type-hinted nor initialized.
questions.explanation: Port randomization is widely used in port scanners to evade detection. By default, Nmap randomizes the scanned port order (except that certain commonly accessible ports are moved near the beginning for efficiency reasons). Scanning ports in sequential numerical order (1-1024) is a pattern that IDS systems can easily detect as a port scan.

questions.question: Which of the following protocols would a penetration tester most likely utilize to exfiltrate data covertly and evade detection?
questions.explanation: DNS (Domain Name System) is commonly used for covert data exfiltration because DNS traffic is typically allowed through firewalls and is not closely monitored. Attackers can use DNS tunneling to encode data within DNS queries and responses, making it appear as legitimate DNS traffic.

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for logical addressing and routing?
questions.explanation: Layer 3 (Network layer) handles logical addressing (IP) and routing between networks.

questions.question: What is the default subnet mask for a Class C network?
questions.explanation: Class C networks use 255.255.255.0 (/24), providing 256 addresses with 254 usable hosts.

questions.question: Which protocol uses port 22 by default?
questions.explanation: SSH (Secure Shell) uses port 22 for encrypted remote administration. Telnet uses port 23 (unencrypted).

questions.question: What does the 'S' in HTTPS stand for?
questions.explanation: HTTPS = HTTP Secure. It uses TLS/SSL encryption to protect data in transit.

questions.question: Which IP address range is reserved for private networks?
What is the purpose of a default gateway?
options: B. Route traffic to different networks
C. Convert domain names to IP addresses
questions.explanation: The default gateway routes traffic from the local network to other networks, including the internet.

questions.question: Which DNS record type maps a domain name to an IPv4 address?
questions.explanation: A record maps hostname to IPv4. AAAA maps to IPv6, MX to mail servers, CNAME is an alias.

questions.question: What is the difference between TCP and UDP?
options: A. TCP is faster than UDP
B. UDP is connection-oriented, TCP is connectionless
C. TCP provides reliable delivery, UDP does not
questions.explanation: TCP is connection-oriented with acknowledgments and retransmission (reliable). UDP is connectionless and faster but unreliable.

questions.question: Which device operates at Layer 2 of the OSI model and uses MAC addresses?
questions.explanation: Switches operate at Layer 2 (Data Link) using MAC addresses to forward frames. Routers use IP (Layer 3).

questions.question: What does VLAN stand for?
questions.explanation: VLAN (Virtual LAN) logically segments a physical network into separate broadcast domains.

questions.question: Which cable type is used to connect a PC to a switch?
questions.explanation: Straight-through cable connects different device types (PC to switch). Crossover connects similar devices.

questions.question: What is the maximum speed of Cat6a Ethernet cable?
questions.explanation: Cat6a supports 10 Gbps up to 100 meters. Cat5e supports 1 Gbps, Cat8 supports 25-40 Gbps.

questions.question: Which wireless standard operates in the 5 GHz band?
questions.explanation: 802.11a and 802.11ac operate on 5 GHz. 802.11b/g use 2.4 GHz. 802.11n supports both bands.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a load balancer?
options: B. Distribute traffic across multiple servers
questions.explanation: Load balancers distribute incoming traffic across multiple backend servers for high availability and performance.

questions.question: Which port does DNS use?
questions.explanation: DNS uses UDP port 53 for queries and TCP port 53 for zone transfers.

questions.question: Which command displays the routing table on a Windows system?
questions.explanation: Both 'route print' and 'netstat -r' display the routing table on Windows. 'ip route' is for Linux.

questions.question: What does DHCP stand for?
questions.explanation: DHCP automatically assigns IP addresses, subnet masks, gateways, and DNS servers to devices.

questions.question: Which tool is used to test connectivity between two network devices?
questions.explanation: ping tests basic connectivity using ICMP echo requests. traceroute shows the route/path taken.

questions.question: What is the purpose of SNMP?
options: B. Monitor and manage network devices
questions.explanation: SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol) monitors network devices like routers, switches, and servers.

questions.question: Which protocol is used for secure file transfer?
questions.explanation: SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) encrypts file transfers over SSH. FTP and TFTP are unencrypted.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a firewall?
options: B. Filter traffic based on rules
C. Assign IP addresses to devices
questions.explanation: Firewalls filter network traffic based on configured rules, allowing or blocking specific connections.

questions.question: Which VPN protocol uses SSL/TLS for encryption?
questions.explanation: OpenVPN uses SSL/TLS for encryption. PPTP is less secure. L2TP/IPsec uses IPsec for encryption.

questions.question: What does WPA3 improve over WPA2?
options: B. Stronger encryption and better authentication
questions.explanation: WPA3 offers SAE (Simultaneous Authentication of Equals), individualized data encryption, and stronger brute-force protection.

questions.question: What is a DMZ in network security?
options: B. A demilitarized zone for publicly accessible servers
questions.explanation: DMZ (Demilitarized Zone) hosts public-facing servers (web, mail, DNS) between the internet and internal network.

questions.question: Which authentication method is most secure for WiFi?
questions.explanation: WPA2/WPA3 Enterprise with 802.1X and RADIUS provides per-user authentication, the most secure WiFi option.

questions.question: A user cannot access the internet but can access local resources. What is the most likely cause?
questions.explanation: If local resources work but internet doesn't, the default gateway (router) is likely misconfigured or unreachable.

questions.question: Which command shows active network connections on Linux?
questions.explanation: ss -tlnp shows TCP listening sockets with process info. ipconfig is Windows. ping tests connectivity.

questions.question: What does 'Destination Host Unreachable' from ping indicate?
options: A. The target host is down
B. No route exists to the destination network
questions.explanation: This error means the local router doesn't know how to reach the destination network (routing issue).

questions.question: A network has intermittent connectivity issues. Which tool would best identify the problematic network segment?
questions.explanation: traceroute (tracert on Windows) shows each hop along the path, helping identify where packets are lost or delayed.

questions.question: Users report slow network performance during peak hours. What is the most likely cause?
questions.explanation: Slow performance during peak hours typically indicates bandwidth saturation — too much traffic for the available capacity.

questions.question: Which wireless charging standard uses magnetic induction?
questions.explanation: Qi (pronounced 'chee') is the most common wireless charging standard, using magnetic induction to transfer power.

questions.question: What does GPS stand for?
questions.explanation: GPS (Global Positioning System) uses satellites to determine precise geographic location.

questions.question: Which display technology uses organic compounds that emit light when an electric current passes through?
questions.explanation: OLED (Organic Light-Emitting Diode) uses organic compounds that emit light when current flows, providing better contrast than LCD.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a digitizer in a touchscreen device?
options: A. Display images on the screen
B. Convert touch input into digital signals
questions.explanation: The digitizer is a layer over the display that detects touch input and converts it into digital signals the device can process.

questions.question: Which mobile device feature uses accelerometers and gyroscopes?
questions.explanation: Accelerometers detect orientation/tilt, gyroscopes detect rotation. Together they enable auto-rotate, gaming motion controls, and step counting.

questions.question: What is the maximum length of a Cat5e Ethernet cable run?
questions.explanation: The maximum recommended length for Cat5e/Cat6 Ethernet cable is 100 meters (328 feet) per the IEEE 802.3 standard.

questions.question: Which connector type is used for fiber optic cables?
questions.explanation: LC (Lucent Connector) and SC (Subscriber Connector) are standard fiber optic connectors. RJ-45 is for copper Ethernet.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a MAC address?
options: A. Identify a device on the internet
B. Uniquely identify a network interface card
C. Assign an IP address automatically
questions.explanation: MAC (Media Access Control) address is a unique hardware identifier burned into every network interface card (NIC).

questions.question: Which wireless standard provides the fastest speed?
questions.explanation: 802.11ax (WiFi 6) offers up to 9.6 Gbps theoretical speed, faster than 802.11ac (WiFi 5) at 3.5 Gbps.

questions.question: What does DHCP dynamically assign to devices?
questions.explanation: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) automatically assigns IP addresses, subnet masks, gateways, and DNS to devices on a network.

questions.question: What does RAM stand for?
questions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is volatile memory used for temporary data storage while the computer is running.

questions.question: Which CPU component performs arithmetic and logical operations?
questions.explanation: The ALU performs arithmetic (add, subtract) and logical (AND, OR, NOT) operations. The Control Unit manages instruction execution.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a UPS?
options: B. Provide backup power during outages
questions.explanation: UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply) provides battery backup power during electrical outages, allowing graceful shutdown.

questions.question: Which storage type has no moving parts and uses flash memory?
questions.explanation: SSD (Solid State Drive) uses flash memory with no moving parts, providing faster access and better durability than HDDs.

questions.question: What does the PSU do in a computer?
options: B. Convert AC power to DC power for components
questions.explanation: PSU (Power Supply Unit) converts AC wall power to various DC voltages (+12V, +5V, +3.3V) needed by computer components.

questions.question: Which port is commonly used for modern video output on graphics cards?
questions.explanation: Modern graphics cards typically include HDMI, DisplayPort, DVI, and sometimes VGA ports for video output.

questions.question: What is the function of a heatsink?
options: B. Dissipate heat from the CPU/GPU
questions.explanation: A heatsink draws heat away from hot components (CPU, GPU) using metal fins that radiate heat into the air.

questions.question: What does the POST sequence do?
options: B. Test hardware components before boot
questions.explanation: POST (Power-On Self Test) checks critical hardware (RAM, CPU, GPU) before the system boots. Beeps indicate errors.

questions.question: What is a virtual machine?
options: B. A software emulation of a computer system
questions.explanation: A VM is a software-based emulation of a physical computer, running an OS and applications like a real machine.

questions.question: Which cloud model provides services over the internet to the general public?
questions.explanation: Public cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP) provides services to anyone over the internet. Private cloud is for single organization use.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of IaaS?
options: A. Pre-built applications ready to use
B. Virtualized computing resources over the internet
questions.explanation: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) provides virtualized computing resources (servers, storage, networking) over the internet.

questions.question: Which Windows command opens the System Configuration utility?
questions.explanation: msconfig (System Configuration) manages startup programs, boot options, and services. regedit is for the registry.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Windows Task Manager?
options: B. View and manage running processes and performance
questions.explanation: Task Manager shows running processes, CPU/memory usage, startup programs, and allows terminating unresponsive applications.

questions.question: Which Linux command displays disk usage of directories?
questions.explanation: du -sh (disk usage, summarize, human-readable) shows the size of directories. df shows filesystem free space.

questions.question: What is the purpose of multifactor authentication?
options: B. Add extra layers of security beyond passwords
questions.explanation: MFA requires multiple verification factors (something you know, have, or are) for stronger security than passwords alone.

questions.question: Which malware type encrypts user files and demands payment for decryption?
questions.explanation: Ransomware encrypts victim's files and demands a ransom payment (usually cryptocurrency) for the decryption key.

questions.question: What does a firewall primarily do?
questions.explanation: Firewalls monitor and filter incoming/outgoing network traffic based on security rules, blocking unauthorized access.

questions.question: A user reports that an application crashes on startup. What is the first troubleshooting step?
options: B. Restart the application and check for error messages
questions.explanation: Always start with the simplest solution: restart the application, check error messages, and identify the specific issue before taking drastic measures.

questions.question: Which Windows tool can be used to check for corrupted system files?
questions.explanation: sfc /scannow (System File Checker) scans and repairs corrupted Windows system files. chkdsk checks disk integrity.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Change Management in IT?
options: B. Control and document changes to minimize risk
questions.explanation: Change Management processes ensure IT changes are planned, tested, approved, and documented to minimize service disruptions.

questions.question: In a Bash script, which Boolean operator would you use to evaluate whether two conditions are both true?
In a script, you need to store multiple values and associate each one with a unique key for easy retrieval. Which data structure would you use?

You are creating a script to automate a series of tasks on a Linux system. You want to check whether a file exists in a directory and take different actions depending on whether it does or doesn’t. Which of the following conditional statements would you use in your script?
In a software development project, which of the following best describes a library?
options: A. A set of related source code files that define objects and their methods
B. A collection of compiled object files that can be linked into an application
C. A hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes, with a root node at the top
D. A set of reusable code modules that can be used in multiple projects

questions.question: A penetration tester is developing a script that will scan multiple ports of a target system. Which of the following loops is the BEST fit for this scenario?

A penetration tester is writing a script to automate a particular task during a penetration test. Which of the following data structures can the tester use to store data in a hierarchical structure that can be easily searched and sorted?
A penetration tester is reviewing the output of a tool and notices that the data is in JSON format. What is JSON?
options: C. A data structure used in machine learning
D. A markup language used for web development

questions.question: During a penetration testing engagement, a tester finds that the web application they are testing uses a dictionary to store user passwords. Which of the following best describes a dictionary?
options: A. A collection of key-value pairs
B. A linear collection of elements
C. A way to organize related data
D. A collection of elements with no order

questions.question: You are working on a penetration testing project for a company that uses Python. Which of the following best describes a class in Python?
options: A. A module that contains a set of related functions or variables
B. A data structure that consists of key-value pairs
C. A way of organizing and creating objects that share common attributes and methods
D. A data structure that consists of a sequence of elements

questions.question: In a scripting language, which string operator can be used to concatenate two strings?

A company is considering outsourcing some of its IT operations to a third-party provider. The provider has offered a master service agreement (MSA) for the company to review. Which of the following best describes the purpose of an MSA in this context?
options: A. To outline the specific services the provider will offer
B. To define the roles and responsibilities of both the company and the provider
C. To establish the price and payment terms for the services
D. To ensure that the provider will comply with all applicable laws and regulations

questions.question: In a penetration testing report, which of the following statements best describes the purpose of the executive summary?

options: A. To provide legal coverage to the tester
B. To identify vulnerabilities in the client's network
C. To ensure that the client's assets are secure

questions.question: In a recent penetration testing engagement, you found that a company’s remote access portal is vulnerable to password-guessing attacks. Which of the following remediation actions should you recommend to the company to improve the security of the remote access portal?
options: A. The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
B. The Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP)
C. The Center for Internet Security (CIS)
D. The International Organization for Standardization (ISO)

questions.question: During a penetration testing engagement, the tester discovered that the target system is vulnerable to a known exploit that could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code. The tester also found that the system does not have a secure configuration and several unnecessary services are running. Which of the following remediation steps should the tester recommend in the report?

During a penetration test, the tester discovers that the target web application is vulnerable to SQL injection attacks due to a lack of sanitization of user input. The tester has obtained a list of usernames and passwords from the database using this vulnerability. What remediation should be recommended in the report?

You have been hired by a company to conduct a penetration test on their website. You have completed the test and have compiled a report with all the necessary information. Which of the following should you include in the report to help the company understand the potential impact of the vulnerabilities found during testing?
options: A. Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP)
B. National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Cybersecurity Framework

questions.question: You have completed a Penetration Test on a company’s network and you are compiling your final report. Which of the following BEST describes the purpose of including a risk rating in your report, according to a reference framework?

options: A. The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)

D. The Information Systems Security Association (ISSA)

questions.question: You have just completed a penetration test for a large corporation and have generated a comprehensive report that includes detailed findings and remediation recommendations. Which of the following components should you include in the appendix of the report?
options: A. To provide an in-depth analysis of vulnerabilities found in the network
B. To outline the methodologies used to carry out the penetration testing
C. To provide an overview of the testing process and a summary of key findings
D. To detail the technical aspects of the attacks carried out during the test

questions.question: In a penetration testing report, you found that a company’s SSH keys have not been rotated in over a year. What remediation would you recommend to address this issue?
options: A. To identify areas where additional testing is needed
B. To create a detailed and accurate report
C. To provide evidence for legal purposes
D. To justify additional testing to the client

questions.question: During a penetration testing engagement, you identify several critical vulnerabilities in a client’s network that need to be addressed. As part of the final report, what should be included in the remediation section?
options: B. Deploy a web application firewall
C. Disable the remote access portal

questions.question: What is an important consideration when writing a penetration testing report for the C-suite?
options: A. Install an antivirus software to prevent the exploit from being executed
B. Disable the unnecessary services to reduce the attack surface
C. Update the exploit signature database to detect the attack

questions.question: In a penetration testing report, which section is used to define the specific assets, systems, and infrastructure that are included in the test, as well as the limitations on the test’s scope?

The tester needs a tool that can perform brute-force attacks, dictionary attacks, and hybrid attacks against password hashes. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a cloud penetration test on a client’s Azure environment and needs to identify any weak passwords that could be exploited by attackers. The tester needs a tool that can scan the environment for misconfigurations and report on any vulnerabilities found. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a white-box penetration test on a target system and needs to test the system’s ability to detect steganography. The tester needs a tool that can be used to detect the presence of hidden data in audio files. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a white-box penetration test on a target system and needs to test the system’s ability to detect steganography. The tester needs a tool that can be used to detect the presence of hidden data in image files. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a network penetration test on a target network and needs to perform a TCP/IP stack fingerprinting to determine the operating system of the target system. The tester needs a tool that can send custom packets and analyze the responses to determine the operating system of the target system. Which of the following tools would best fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a web application penetration test on a target web application and needs to identify vulnerabilities such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS). The tester needs a tool that can scan the web application for vulnerabilities and provide detailed information on how to remediate them. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

The tester needs a tool that can exploit a vulnerability in the WPS protocol to recover the network’s WPS PIN. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a wireless penetration test on a target network and needs to crack the network’s Wi-Fi password. The tester needs a tool that can capture wireless traffic and use it to crack the password. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a wireless penetration test on a target network and needs to identify the wireless devices within range of the tester’s device. The tester needs a tool that can scan for wireless devices and display detailed information about them, including their MAC address and manufacturer. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing reconnaissance on a target organization and needs to gather information about its online presence, including its website, social media profiles, and other online properties. The tester needs a tool that can query public databases and display detailed information about the target organization’s online presence in a visual graph format. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing reconnaissance on a target domain and needs to gather information about the domain’s ownership and registration details. The tester needs a tool that can query the domain registration database and display detailed information about the domain owner, including contact details and registration history. Which of the following tools would best fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing reconnaissance on a target domain and needs to gather information about the domain’s DNS configuration, including its DNS server IP addresses and MX records. The tester needs a tool that can query the domain’s DNS server and display detailed information about its DNS configuration. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a vulnerability assessment of a target system and needs to identify potential exploits for known vulnerabilities in the target system. The tester needs a tool that can search through a database of known exploits and display detailed information about each exploit, including the exploit’s code and usage. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a code review of an application and needs to analyze the application’s assembly code in order to identify potential vulnerabilities or security flaws. The tester needs a tool that can be used to disassemble and debug the application code. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

A penetration tester is performing a web application penetration test and wants to identify common vulnerabilities in the target web server. The tester needs a tool that can identify potential vulnerabilities such as misconfigurations, outdated software, and server-side scripting errors. Which of the following tools would BEST fit this need?

In a penetration test scenario, a tester has discovered an exploit that allows an attacker to enumerate users on a remote system. The following code snippet is found in the exploit code. What is the purpose of this code?
In a penetration test scenario, a tester has identified a code snippet that allows remote access to a target system. Which of the following code snippets could be used to launch remote access?

A penetration tester wants to examine exploit code that is capable of downloading a file from a remote system. Which of the following lines of code would be used to download the file in this scenario?
options: A. This code sends an HTTP GET request to the "/users" endpoint on the remote system and prints the response to the console
B. This code sends an HTTP POST request to the "/users" endpoint on the remote system and returns the response
C. This code sends an HTTP GET request to the "/passwords" endpoint on the remote system and prints the response to the console
D. This code sends an HTTP GET request to the "/users" endpoint on the local system and prints the response to the console

questions.question: In a penetration test scenario, a tester wants to send an HTTP GET request to a target website and receive a response. Which of the following Python code snippets will accomplish this task?
In a penetration test scenario, a tester wants to brute force a login page with a list of usernames and passwords. Which of the following Python code snippets will accomplish this task?
A tester wants to create a script to scan a network range and identify hosts with port 22 open. Which of the following code snippets written in Ruby will accomplish this task?
options: A. import requests target_url = "http://www.example.com" response = requests.get(target_url) print(response.text)
B. import urllib.request target_url = "http://www.example.com" response = urllib.request.urlopen(target_url) print(response.read())

C. import http.client target_host = "www.example.com" target_port = 80 conn = http.client.HTTPConnection(target_host, target_port) conn.request("GET", "/") response = conn.getresponse() print(response.read())

D. import socket target_host = "www.example.com" target_port = 80 client = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) client.connect((target_host, target_port)) client.send("GET / HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com

") response = client.recv(4096) print(response)

questions.question: A penetration tester has found a PowerShell script on a target system that appears to be running a series of commands to enumerate system information. Which of the following commands would the tester expect to find in the script?
options: A. import requests url = "https://www.example.com/login" usernames = ["user1", "user2", "user3"] passwords = ["pass1", "pass2", "pass3"] for username in usernames: for password in passwords: data = {"username": username, "password": password} response = requests.post(url, data=data) if "Login failed" not in response.text: print("Successful login: {} - {}".format(username, password)) break

B. import requests url = "https://www.example.com/login" usernames = ["user1", "user2", "user3"] passwords = ["pass1", "pass2", "pass3"] for username in usernames: for password in passwords: data = {"username": username, "password": password} response = requests.post(url, data=data) if "Login successful" in response.text: print("Successful login: {} - {}".format(username, password)) break

C. import requests url = "https://www.example.com/login" usernames = ["user1", "user2", "user3"] passwords = ["pass1", "pass2", "pass3"] for username in usernames: for password in passwords: data = {"username": username, "password": password} response = requests.post(url, data=data) if response.status_code == 200: print("Successful login: {} - {}".format(username, password)) break

D. import requests url = "https://www.example.com/login" usernames = ["user1", "user2", "user3"] passwords = ["pass1", "pass2", "pass3"] for username in usernames: for password in passwords: data = {"username": username, "password": password} response = requests.post(url, data=data) if response.ok: print("Successful login: {} - {}".format(username, password)) break

questions.question: As a penetration tester, you have identified a vulnerable machine and have successfully gained access to it through a remote exploit. You have discovered that the system uses a bash shell, and you want to create a backdoor that will allow you to maintain access to the system after you’ve completed your testing. What command can be used to create a bash backdoor that will allow you to maintain access to a compromised system?
options: A. require 'socket' (1..255).each do |i| begin Timeout::timeout(1) { TCPSocket.new("192.168.1.#{i}", 22) } puts "192.168.1.#{i} has port 22 open" rescue end end

B. require 'nmap/xml' nmap = Nmap::XML.new `sudo nmap -sT -p 22 192.168.1.0/24` nmap.each_host do |host| if host.up? host.each_port do |port| puts "#{host.addr} has port #{port.number} open" end end end

C. require 'socket' hosts = %w[192.168.1.1 192.168.1.2 192.168.1.3 192.168.1.4] hosts.each do |host| begin Timeout::timeout(1) { TCPSocket.new(host, 22) } puts "#{host} has port 22 open" rescue end end

D. require 'net/ssh' hosts = %w[192.168.1.1 192.168.1.2 192.168.1.3 192.168.1.4] hosts.each do |host| begin Net::SSH.start(host, 'username', :password => 'password') do |ssh| puts "#{host} has port 22 open" end rescue end end

questions.question: During a penetration testing engagement, the tester discovered that the client’s employees were not following proper password hygiene practices. As part of the “lessons learned” process, which of the following should the client do to address this issue?

During a penetration testing engagement, a tester utilized various tools to conduct the test. Once the test is complete, what should be the next step for the tester in regard to the tools used during the test?

After a recent penetration testing engagement, a security analyst discovers that the penetration testers created some user accounts with elevated privileges to perform testing. What post-engagement cleanup activity should the analyst perform to ensure the removal of these accounts and their associated access rights?
options: A. Implement a mandatory password change policy every six months
B. Provide training to employees on proper password hygiene practices
C. Hire a third-party security firm to conduct regular penetration testing
D. Monitor all employee activity on the company network

questions.question: After conducting a penetration test on a client’s network, the tester set up a reverse shell to maintain access to the target system. Which of the following is a recommended post-engagement cleanup activity to remove the reverse shell?
options: A. The tester should delete all the tools and binaries used during the engagement
B. The tester should keep the tools and binaries used during the engagement for future engagements
C. The tester should move the tools and binaries used during the engagement to a shared drive
D. The tester should encrypt the tools and binaries used during the engagement

questions.question: In a penetration testing engagement, the tester discovers a critical vulnerability that could potentially cause severe damage to the client’s system. The client is reluctant to address the issue, fearing that it would cause major disruptions in their operations. What should the penetration tester do in this situation?
options: A. Update the access control list (ACL) for the user accounts to revoke the elevated privileges
B. Notify the system administrator to remove the accounts and revoke the associated privileges
C. Change the password for the user account to an invalid value
D. Delete the user accounts and revoke the associated privileges

questions.question: During a penetration testing engagement, you have found a critical vulnerability that could result in a major data breach. Who should you communicate this finding to, and in what manner?
options: A. Delete the compromised user account
B. Remove the malware from the system
C. Reinstall the operating system on the target system
D. Kill the reverse shell process and delete any associated files

questions.question: In a penetration testing engagement, who should be the technical contact for the pentester to communicate with regarding technical issues and vulnerabilities discovered during testing?
options: A. Continue the engagement and notify the client of the findings in the final report
B. Escalate the issue to the client's senior management and regulatory authorities
C. Explain the severity of the issue and the consequences of inaction to the client, encouraging them to address the issue immediately
D. Wait for the client to decide on the remediation plan

questions.question: In a penetration testing project, who is the primary contact for the penetration tester to communicate with?
options: A. You should communicate this finding to both the technical and primary contacts, as it is a critical finding that could impact the security of the entire organization. You should use a secure communication method, such as encrypted email or a secure messaging platform

B. You should communicate this finding to the technical contact only, as they are the ones responsible for implementing technical controls. You should use a non-secure communication method, such as phone or email

C. You should communicate this finding to the primary contact only, as they are the ones responsible for making decisions regarding the organization's security posture. You should use a secure communication method, such as encrypted email or a secure messaging platform
D. You should not communicate this finding to anyone, as it could potentially cause damage to the organization

questions.question: In a company’s server room, a security auditor discovers that one of the servers is missing, and there is no record of it being decommissioned. The server contained sensitive data, including customers’ financial information. Which remediation would the auditor MOST likely recommend in the report?

In a penetration test scenario, a tester wants to write a script that will automate the process of submitting a login form with a list of usernames and passwords. The script will read the usernames and passwords from a file and submit them to the form until a successful login is achieved. Which of the following JavaScript code snippets will accomplish this task?
options: B. The CEO of the organization

questions.question: In a penetration testing engagement, you have successfully gained access to a workstation within the target organization. You obtained a password hash of the domain administrator account from the workstation’s memory. What is the next step in performing a Pass the Hash attack?
options: A. Install biometric controls in the server room
B. Conduct mandatory vacations for IT staff
C. Implement video surveillance in the server room
D. Implement role-based access control to the server room

questions.question: In a penetration testing engagement, you have successfully gained access to an endpoint as a low-privileged user. You have identified a process running on the system that is used by a high-privileged user. Which of the following methods can you use for horizontal privilege escalation?
options: A. const usernames = ["user1", "user2", "user3"]; const passwords = ["pass1", "pass2", "pass3"]; for (let i = 0; i < usernames.length; i++) { for (let j = 0; j < passwords.length; j++) { document.querySelector("#username").value = usernames[i]; document.querySelector("#password").value = passwords[j]; document.querySelector("form").submit(); } }

B. const fs = require("fs"); const fileContents = fs.readFileSync("loginData.txt", "utf-8"); const credentials = fileContents.split("\n"); for (let i = 0; i < credentials.length; i++) { const [username, password] = credentials[i].split(":"); document.querySelector("#username").value = username; document.querySelector("#password").value = password; document.querySelector("form").submit(); }

C. const usernames = ["user1", "user2", "user3"]; const passwords = ["pass1", "pass2", "pass3"]; usernames.forEach(username => { passwords.forEach(password => { document.querySelector("#username").value = username; document.querySelector("#password").value = password; document.querySelector("form").submit(); }); });

D. const credentials = [ { username: "user1", password: "pass1" }, { username: "user2", password: "pass2" }, { username: "user3", password: "pass3" } ]; credentials.forEach(credential => { document.querySelector("#username").value = credential.username; document.querySelector("#password").value = credential.password; document.querySelector("form").submit(); });

questions.question: A pen tester has successfully installed a trojan on a target system to create a persistent foothold. However, the trojan was detected and removed by the system’s antivirus software. The pen tester needs to find a way to reinstall the trojan without being detected. B. Use the password hash to authenticate to other systems and escalate privileges

options: A. Add additional authentication factors to the vulnerable operations

questions.question: In a pen test engagement, a security analyst discovers that a smart thermostat communicates with the cloud service provider using HTTP instead of HTTPS. Which of the following is the MOST likely impact of this vulnerability?

A company has been using the same IoT devices for several years without updating the firmware or hardware. A security researcher discovered a critical vulnerability that could allow an attacker to gain unauthorized access to the devices. Which of the following is the BEST course of action?

questions.question: A water treatment plant has implemented a new SCADA system to monitor the water levels, temperature, and other parameters. What is whaling in social engineering attacks?
